content: GBMs produce distorted, often overconfident probability estimates needing post-hoc calibration. Platt/sigmoid assumes sigmoid distortion; isotonic is non-parametric, handles irregular distortion but needs more data and overfits with little data. For GBMs specifically Platt is often a poor fit (DSSE: calibration worsening model). Niculescu-Mizil Caruana ICML05: boosted trees tend to require calibration, isotonic corrects high-variance distortion Platt cannot. Calibration drifts under covariate shift, recalibrate on fresh data, use held-out calibration set to avoid leakage. Always inspect reliability plot first.
